skill.name: tdd-guide
skill.description: Guide test-driven development with generated test plans, framework-specific stubs, coverage parsing, and test quality analysis.
skill.summary: Generate test plans, coverage insights, and TDD workflow guidance for engineering teams.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found no command execution, credential exfiltration, or malicious network behavior. Most alerts are false positives from documentation code fences, placeholder installation paths, example URLs, and testing terminology. The remaining risk is that scripts generate executable test code from user-controlled names and text, so generated output should be reviewed before use.

medium_findings.title: Generated Test Code Uses Unescaped User-Controlled Text
medium_findings.description: The skill generates executable test source by interpolating suite names, test names, descriptions, module names, and test bodies. This is expected for a code generation skill, but unsafe text can alter generated tests if output is executed without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly interpolates user-derived values into generated source strings. The risk is bounded because the skill produces output for developer review and does not execute it itself.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Blocks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged markdown backticks and example shell commands as Ruby or shell execution. These are documentation snippets for installation, validation, and workflow examples, not executable code paths in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review found no subprocess, os.system, popen, eval, or exec calls. The flagged text is inside markdown examples and skill prompts.

low_findings.title: Example URLs and FTP Strings Are Test Data
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs and the FTP string appear in repository clone instructions and boundary-value fixture data. No Python module imports a network client or sends data to these addresses.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are literal examples and fixture boundary values. No requests, urllib, socket, curl invocation, or other network execution path was found.

low_findings.title: Hidden Directory and API Key Alerts Are Documentation Placeholders
low_findings.description: References to .claude paths document skill installation locations, and the API key text is a placeholder for API usage. No evidence found that the skill reads hidden directories or environment secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are install or API examples and a prior evaluation note. The reviewed Python files do not access environment variables or secret files.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance and Weak Crypto Keywords Are Testing Terminology
low_findings.description: Scanner hits for reconnaissance, C2, and weak cryptography come from coverage analysis terms, format detection, password examples, token examples, and test names. No evidence found of malicious discovery, command-and-control behavior, or cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is test generation, coverage parsing, and sample password/token validation. No malicious protocol, beaconing, or crypto code was identified.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unescaped Source Generation Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: Generated test files embed module names and test descriptions into import statements, test names, comments, and docstrings. This can produce unsafe or malformed code when untrusted input contains quotes, path segments, or code syntax.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The string interpolation is directly visible at the cited locations. The skill does not execute the output, which limits impact.

content.user_title: Guide TDD and Generate Test Plans
content.value_statement: Teams often miss edge cases when writing tests under time pressure. This skill guides TDD cycles, creates test scaffolds, and explains coverage gaps across common frameworks.
actual_capabilities: Generates test case outlines from user stories, acceptance criteria, and API specifications.
Creates Jest, Pytest, JUnit, Vitest, Mocha, unittest, and TestNG style test scaffolds.
Parses LCOV, JSON, and XML coverage reports to summarize line, branch, and function coverage.
Identifies missing test scenarios from branches, error handlers, validation rules, and edge cases.
Scores test quality using assertion counts, naming quality, isolation signals, and common test smells.
Guides red, green, and refactor phases with validation checklists and next-step prompts.
limitations: It does not execute tests or verify runtime behavior.
Generated test code needs human review before being added to a repository.
Coverage analysis depends on supplied LCOV, JSON, or XML reports.
Support is strongest for TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, and Java projects.

use_cases.title: Plan Tests for a New Feature
use_cases.description: Turn requirements into focused happy-path, error, and edge-case test scenarios before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Improve Coverage Before Release
use_cases.description: Review coverage reports and prioritize missing tests for risky branches, validation paths, and error handling.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team TDD Practice
use_cases.description: Use consistent red, green, and refactor guidance during pairing, onboarding, and code review preparation.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Basic Unit Tests
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one function or class.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use tdd-guide to generate unit test cases for this function. Cover success, validation errors, and boundary values: [paste code].

prompt_templates.title: Start a TDD Cycle
prompt_templates.prompt: Use tdd-guide to guide the red phase for this requirement. Give me failing test scenarios first: [describe requirement].

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Coverage Gaps
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review using existing coverage data.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use tdd-guide to analyze this coverage report and source context. Prioritize uncovered branches and error paths: [paste report and notes].

prompt_templates.title: Migrate Test Frameworks
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced framework conversion with quality review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use tdd-guide to convert these tests to another framework. Preserve behavior and note any setup, fixture, or assertion changes: [paste tests].

output_examples.input: Create tests for a password validation function with length and complexity rules.
output: A prioritized test plan with happy path, missing character classes, minimum length, and empty input cases.
Framework-specific scaffold suggestions with clear arrange, act, and assert sections.
Notes about reviewing generated test names and fixture values before execution.

output_examples.input: Analyze an LCOV report for an authentication module.
output: A coverage summary showing line, branch, and function coverage percentages.
Priority recommendations for uncovered invalid credential, expired token, and lockout paths.
A concise list of tests that would close the highest-risk gaps.

output_examples.input: Guide TDD for adding user registration.
output: A red-phase checklist focused on email validation, password rules, and duplicate account behavior.
Green-phase guidance for minimal implementation after failing tests are confirmed.
Refactor prompts that preserve passing tests while improving naming and duplication.

best_practices: Provide requirements, expected behavior, and existing framework conventions before asking for generated tests.
Review all generated test code before saving or running it in a project.
Use coverage reports with source context so recommendations focus on meaningful gaps.
anti_patterns: Do not run generated tests blindly when prompts include untrusted source text.
Do not chase coverage percentages without checking assertion quality and critical paths.
Do not use broad tests that combine many behaviors into one failure point.

faq.question: Which testing frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: The skill includes patterns for Jest, Vitest, Mocha, Pytest, unittest, JUnit, TestNG, and Jasmine.

faq.question: Can it run my test suite?
faq.answer: No. It generates guidance, scaffolds, and analysis. You run tests with your normal project tools.

faq.question: What coverage formats can it read?
faq.answer: It supports LCOV, Istanbul-style JSON, and Cobertura-style XML coverage data.

faq.question: Does it modify my repository files?
faq.answer: No evidence found that it writes files. It produces text that users can review and apply.

faq.question: Is generated code safe to execute?
faq.answer: Review it first, especially when source code, test names, or requirements come from untrusted input.

faq.question: What languages work best?
faq.answer: The strongest support is for TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, and Java projects.
